label: Privacy Policy
title: CycleForge Privacy Policy
md:
# CycleForge Privacy Policy

> **DRAFT — NOT YET LEGAL ADVICE.** This document is a detailed working draft prepared to reflect CycleForge's actual architecture and data practices. It has **not** been reviewed by a licensed attorney and is **not** legal advice. Bracketed placeholders (e.g. `[LEGAL ENTITY NAME]`, `[GOVERNING-LAW STATE]`) must be completed, and the whole document reviewed against your jurisdiction(s) and regulatory obligations, before you publish it or rely on it.

*Last updated: June 23, 2026 · Effective: [EFFECTIVE DATE].*

---

## 1. Introduction & Scope

This Privacy Policy (the "**Policy**") explains how **[LEGAL ENTITY NAME]**, located at **[REGISTERED ADDRESS]** ("**CycleForge**", "**we**", "**us**", "**our**"), collects, uses, discloses, and protects personal information in connection with the **CycleForge** service — a visual operations platform for used-goods resellers covering receiving, testing and grading, inventory, multi-channel listing and fulfillment, and returns/warranty, modeled as a real-time, audit-logged, scan-first node-graph workflow (the "**Service**") — and the marketing website at **cycleforge.com** (the "**Site**"). The Service is offered self-serve at **$50 per month per workspace** with a 14-day free trial.

This Policy should be read together with our sibling documents — the **Terms of Service**, the **Data Processing Agreement** ("**DPA**"), and the **Amazon Selling Partner Data Protection Notice** in Section 5 — which together govern your use of the Service.

**Who this Policy concerns.** Several distinct categories of people interact with CycleForge, and our role differs for each:

- **The Customer** — the business (reseller) that subscribes to a workspace under the Terms of Service ("**you**", "**Customer**").
- **Authorized Users** — the Customer's staff and operators who access a workspace through PIN, passkey, single sign-on (SSO), or email magic-link authentication ("**Authorized Users**").
- **End-customers / buyers** — the Customer's own buyers and walk-in customers whose personal information appears in the Customer's business records (e.g. names and shipping addresses on marketplace orders). CycleForge does not have a direct relationship with these individuals.

**Controller vs. processor split.** Data-protection law distinguishes the party that determines the purposes and means of processing (the "**controller**") from the party that processes data on the controller's behalf (the "**processor**").

| Data domain | CycleForge's role | Controller |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Account & profile data** (organization, Authorized User names/emails, credentials, roles, sessions, billing contact, audit logs) | **Controller** | CycleForge |
| **Usage & telemetry** (analytics, performance/error logs, scan events, IP, device) | **Controller** | CycleForge |
| **Customer Data** (the reseller's business records, including end-customer/buyer personal data) | **Processor** | The Customer |

For all **Customer Data**, the Customer is the controller and CycleForge acts solely as a processor under the **DPA** and on the Customer's documented instructions. Where this Policy addresses Customer Data, it describes our handling as a processor; the Customer's own privacy notice governs how that data is collected from and used toward end-customers.

This Policy does **not** cover: (a) third-party services the Customer connects with the Customer's own credentials (see Section 6); or (b) infrastructure the Customer controls, including the Customer's own NAS (Synology / UGREEN) photo storage over WebDAV and the Customer's own local Ollama LLM reached through the Customer's Cloudflare tunnel — CycleForge does not host or access these.

---

## 2. Information We Collect

We collect the following categories of information.

### (a) Account & profile data — *controller*

Collected when a workspace is created and as Authorized Users are added and use the Service:

- **Organization** name and slug.
- **Authorized User** names and email addresses.
- **Authentication credentials** — hashed PINs (no plaintext is ever stored) and passkey (WebAuthn) credentials; for SSO and email magic-link sign-in, the associated email identifier.
- **Roles and permissions** assigned via our role-based access control (RBAC) and permission registry.
- **Sessions and devices** — session records and device/user-agent metadata.
- **Billing contact** for the workspace and the active **plan/subscription** status.
- **Audit logs** — an append-only record of significant actions taken in the workspace (who did what, when), used for security and accountability.

### (b) Billing data — *controller*

Subscription billing and payment processing are handled by **Stripe, Inc.** We **never store full card numbers**. From Stripe we receive and store only limited billing metadata — the billing contact, plan, and a payment-method descriptor such as the card brand, **last four digits, and expiry** — for invoicing, receipts, dunning, and fraud prevention. Card data is collected directly by Stripe under its PCI-DSS-compliant environment.

### (c) Customer Data, including end-customer/buyer personal data — *processor*

"**Customer Data**" is the business data a Customer and its Authorized Users create in, upload to, or import into the Service, processed by CycleForge on the Customer's behalf. It includes:

- **Inventory** records — SKUs, serial numbers, condition grades, locations, quantities, and product/receiving photos.
- **Purchase orders** and supplier data.
- **Sales orders** and line items.
- **End-customer / buyer personal data** — names, shipping addresses, email addresses, and phone numbers — imported from connected marketplaces and recorded for walk-in sales.
- **Tracking numbers**, returns/warranty records, and support-ticket content.

Customer Data is ingested both from data the Customer enters directly (scan-first operations, walk-in sales) and from third-party channels the Customer connects with the Customer's own credentials. The sources and the buyer personal data they may carry are:

| Source / connected channel | Connected with | Personal data that may flow into Customer Data |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Amazon Selling Partner API (SP-API)** | Customer's credentials | **Merchant-fulfilled-order buyer PII only**, accessed via the Amazon **Restricted Data Token (RDT)** and only to ship (name, shipping address). **FBA buyer PII is never requested.** See Section 5. |
| **eBay** | Customer's credentials | Buyer name, shipping address, order/line-item data, tracking |
| **Ecwid** | Customer's credentials | Buyer name, shipping address, email, phone, order data |
| **Square** | Customer's credentials | Buyer/customer name, contact details, order data |
| **Zoho (Inventory)** | Customer's credentials | Inventory, supplier, and order records (may include contact data) |
| **Google — Sheets / Gmail / Photos** | Customer's credentials (OAuth) | Data the Customer chooses to import (e.g. order/contact rows, message content, photos) |
| **Zendesk** | Customer's credentials | Support-ticket and warranty content, which may include requester contact details |
| **Shipping carriers — UPS, FedEx, USPS** | Customer's credentials | Tracking numbers and tracking/status data |

We process buyer personal data within Customer Data **only** to provide the Service to the Customer (e.g. so the Customer can pick, pack, ship, and handle returns) and on the Customer's instructions. We do not use it for our own purposes.

### (d) Usage, telemetry, device, and IP data — *controller*

To operate, secure, and improve the Service we collect:

- **Product analytics** — feature usage, funnels, and session context.
- **Performance and error logs**, including diagnostic traces.
- **Scan events** and operational telemetry generated by scan-first workflows.
- **IP address** and **device / user-agent** information, used for security, abuse prevention, and rate-limiting.

### (e) Cookies & analytics

The Site and Service use a small number of cookies and similar technologies, split into **essential** (required for authentication, session integrity, security, and load handling) and **analytics** (product and marketing-site analytics via **PostHog**). See Sections 4 and 11 for legal basis and controls.

---

## 3. How We Use Information

We use the information described above to:

- **Provide and operate the Service** — authenticate Authorized Users, run the node-graph workflow, maintain inventory and order state, render real-time dashboards and scan bridges, and store product/receiving photos.
- **Fulfill orders on the Customer's behalf** — surface buyer shipping details to the relevant Authorized User so the Customer can pick, pack, label, and ship (including via the Amazon RDT flow in Section 5).
- **Process billing** — manage subscriptions, trials, invoicing, receipts, and dunning through Stripe.
- **Provide support** — respond to and troubleshoot requests sent to **hi@cycleforge.ai** and resolve account issues.
- **Maintain security, prevent fraud and abuse, and enforce rate limits** — using IP, device, audit-log, and scan-event signals; maintain per-tenant isolation.
- **Improve the Service and conduct analytics** — understand usage and performance via PostHog and our own logs, and develop new features.
- **Comply with legal obligations** — respond to lawful requests, keep records, and enforce our Terms of Service.
- **Communicate with you** — send service, security, billing, and transactional messages (via **Resend**) and, where permitted, product updates.

We do **not** use Customer Data (including buyer PII) to train generative AI models, and we do not sell or share personal information for cross-context behavioral advertising (see Section 6).

---

## 4. Legal Bases for Processing (GDPR / UK GDPR)

Where the EU General Data Protection Regulation ("**GDPR**") or the UK GDPR ("**UK GDPR**") applies, we rely on the following legal bases for our processing **as a controller** (for Customer Data, the Customer as controller is responsible for establishing the legal basis):

- **Performance of a contract** (Art. 6(1)(b)) — to provide the Service to the Customer and to manage Authorized User accounts, authentication, billing, and support under the Terms of Service.
- **Legitimate interests** (Art. 6(1)(f)) — to secure the Service and prevent fraud and abuse; to apply rate-limiting and maintain availability; to keep audit logs for accountability; to understand product usage and improve and develop the Service; and to send service-related communications. We balance these interests against the rights and freedoms of the individuals concerned.
- **Consent** (Art. 6(1)(a)) — for non-essential **analytics cookies** and similar technologies, and for any optional marketing communications. Consent can be withdrawn at any time (Section 11).
- **Legal obligation** (Art. 6(1)(c)) — to comply with tax, accounting, and other legal requirements and to respond to lawful requests.

---

## 5. Amazon Selling Partner Data — Data Protection Notice

*This Section supplements the Policy and governs all personal information obtained through the Amazon Selling Partner API (SP-API). It is designed to comply with the Amazon Acceptable Use Policy and the Amazon Data Protection Policy.*

When a Customer connects its Amazon seller account using the Customer's own credentials, CycleForge accesses Amazon order information solely to help the Customer fulfill its orders. We adhere to the following commitments:

- **RDT-gated access.** Buyer personally identifiable information ("**PII**") for **merchant-fulfilled orders** is accessed only through the Amazon **Restricted Data Token (RDT)**. We request the minimum scope required and only at the point of fulfillment.
- **Ship-only purpose.** Amazon buyer PII is used **exclusively to ship the relevant order** (e.g. to generate the correct shipping label and pick/pack the order). It is not used for any other purpose.
- **FBA excluded.** We **never request FBA (Fulfilled by Amazon) buyer PII**, because the Customer does not ship those orders.
- **No sale; no marketing.** Amazon buyer PII is **never sold** and is **never shared or used for marketing, advertising, or any secondary purpose**.
- **Encryption.** Amazon data is encrypted **in transit** (TLS 1.2+) and **at rest** (encrypted database storage on Neon; integration credentials encrypted with AES-256-GCM in our application secret vault — see Section 9).
- **Least privilege and isolation.** Access is restricted to authorized personnel and processes on a least-privilege basis, and is isolated per tenant through PostgreSQL Row-Level Security.
- **Retention and deletion.** We retain Amazon PII only as long as necessary to fulfill the order and to meet the Customer's legitimate operational and legal needs, after which it is deleted or de-identified in line with the Amazon Data Protection Policy and the retention practices in Section 8. On the Customer's instruction or account closure, Amazon PII is deleted in accordance with the DPA.
- **Incident response.** Suspected security incidents involving Amazon data are handled under our incident-response process and reported as required (contact **security@cycleforge.ai**).

---

## 6. How We Share Information & Sub-Processors

We share personal information only as described below.

### Infrastructure sub-processors

We engage the following vendors to process data in order to run the Service. These are CycleForge's own sub-processors, engaged under data-protection terms consistent with our obligations:

| Sub-processor | Purpose | Location |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Vercel Inc.** | Application hosting, serverless compute, edge/CDN | USA |
| **Neon Inc.** | Managed serverless PostgreSQL — primary data store (encrypted at rest) | USA |
| **Upstash Inc.** | Managed Redis — rate-limiting, caching, distributed locks | USA |
| **Ably Ltd.** | Realtime pub/sub messaging — live dashboards, scan bridges | UK/EU + USA |
| **Stripe, Inc.** | Subscription billing and payment processing (PCI-DSS; no card numbers stored by CycleForge) | USA |
| **Resend** | Transactional email delivery | USA |
| **Google LLC / Google Cloud** | Google Cloud Storage for product/receiving photos; Google API access for Customer-connected Google integrations | USA |
| **PostHog Inc.** | Product and marketing-site analytics (usage, funnels, session context) | USA / EU |

The current list of sub-processors is maintained for Customers in accordance with the DPA, including notice of changes and the right to object as set out there.

### Customer-connected Integration Partners

Where a Customer connects a third-party service using the **Customer's own credentials**, data flows to and from that service **at the Customer's direction**. These are the **Customer's relationships, not CycleForge sub-processors**: **Amazon SP-API, eBay, Ecwid, Square, Zoho, Google (Sheets / Gmail / Photos), Zendesk**, and the shipping carriers **UPS, FedEx, USPS**. Their handling of personal information is governed by their own terms and privacy policies. Likewise, the Customer's own **NAS** photo storage (WebDAV) and the Customer's own **Ollama** LLM (via the Customer's Cloudflare tunnel) are **Customer-controlled infrastructure** that CycleForge does not host or access.

### Legal, safety, and corporate disclosures

We may disclose personal information: (a) to comply with applicable law, legal process, or a lawful government request; (b) to enforce our Terms of Service or protect the rights, property, or safety of CycleForge, our Customers, or others, and to prevent fraud or abuse; and (c) in connection with a merger, acquisition, financing, reorganization, or sale of assets (a "**business transfer**"), in which case personal information may be transferred subject to this Policy, with notice to affected Customers as required.

### No sale or sharing for advertising (CCPA/CPRA)

**We do not sell, and we do not "share" (as defined under the California Consumer Privacy Act, as amended by the CPRA), personal information for cross-context behavioral advertising.** We have not sold or shared personal information for such purposes in the preceding 12 months.

---

## 7. International Data Transfers

CycleForge is operated, and most of its infrastructure is hosted, in the **United States**. Some sub-processors operate in the USA and, in the case of Ably and PostHog, in the EU/UK as well. Where we transfer personal information of individuals in the European Economic Area, the United Kingdom, or Switzerland to a country that has not received an adequacy decision, we rely on appropriate safeguards — principally the **EU Standard Contractual Clauses ("SCCs")**, supplemented by the **UK International Data Transfer Addendum (the "UK Addendum")** for UK transfers, together with supplementary technical and organizational measures (Section 9). A copy of the relevant transfer mechanism is available on request to **privacy@cycleforge.ai**.

---

## 8. Data Retention

We retain personal information only as long as necessary for the purposes described in this Policy or as required by law.

- **Account & profile data** — retained for the life of the subscription and for a limited **wind-down window** after termination to allow account recovery, export, and final billing, after which it is deleted or de-identified. Audit logs may be retained for the period necessary for security and legal accountability.
- **Billing records** — retained as required for tax, accounting, and audit purposes.
- **Customer Data** (including buyer PII) — retained and deleted **per the DPA and the Customer's instructions**. On termination, the Customer may export Customer Data within the period stated in the DPA, after which we delete or return it as instructed. The Customer, as controller, is responsible for setting retention appropriate to the underlying records.
- **Usage, telemetry, and logs** — retained for a limited period sufficient for security, debugging, and analytics, then deleted or aggregated/de-identified.
- **Amazon PII** — retained and deleted as described in Section 5.

When personal information is no longer needed, we delete it or irreversibly de-identify it.

---

## 9. Security

We maintain technical and organizational measures designed to protect personal information against unauthorized access, disclosure, alteration, and destruction, including:

- **Encryption in transit** — **TLS 1.2+** for all connections.
- **Encryption at rest** — database storage on **Neon** is encrypted at rest.
- **Secret vault** — integration credentials and secrets are encrypted with **AES-256-GCM** in an application secret vault keyed by a KMS-managed key.
- **Hard per-tenant isolation** — **PostgreSQL Row-Level Security (RLS)** enforces tenant isolation under a **non-bypass database role**, and realtime channels are isolated per tenant.
- **Access control** — **role-based access control (RBAC)** with a permission registry and **least-privilege** access for personnel and services.
- **Auditability and integrity** — full **audit logging** and an **idempotent, append-only event ledger**.
- **Abuse resistance** — **rate-limiting** at the application and edge layers.
- **Credential hygiene** — PINs are **hashed** (no plaintext storage); passkeys use WebAuthn credentials.

No method of transmission or storage is completely secure, and we cannot guarantee absolute security. Suspected vulnerabilities or incidents should be reported to **security@cycleforge.ai**; we will notify affected Customers and individuals of personal-data breaches as required by applicable law and the DPA. This Policy does **not** claim any certification (such as SOC 2, ISO 27001, or HIPAA compliance) that we have not separately and expressly stated.

---

## 10. Your Privacy Rights

The rights available to you depend on where you live and your relationship with CycleForge. For **Customer Data**, where CycleForge is a processor, requests from end-customers/buyers are **routed to the Customer** (the controller), who is responsible for responding; we assist the Customer as required under the DPA.

### EU / UK / Swiss residents (GDPR & UK GDPR)

Subject to applicable law, you have the right to:

- **Access** the personal information we hold about you and obtain a copy.
- **Rectify** inaccurate or incomplete personal information.
- **Erase** your personal information ("right to be forgotten").
- **Restrict** processing in certain circumstances.
- **Data portability** — receive your personal information in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format.
- **Object** to processing based on legitimate interests, and to direct marketing at any time.
- **Withdraw consent** at any time where processing is based on consent (e.g. analytics cookies), without affecting prior processing.
- **Lodge a complaint** with your local data-protection supervisory authority (in the UK, the Information Commissioner's Office).

### California residents (CCPA / CPRA)

Subject to applicable law, you have the right to:

- **Know / access** the categories and specific pieces of personal information we have collected, the sources, the purposes, and the categories of recipients.
- **Delete** personal information we have collected, subject to legal exceptions.
- **Correct** inaccurate personal information.
- **Opt out of the "sale" or "sharing"** of personal information — note that, as stated in Section 6, **we do not sell or share personal information**.
- **Limit the use of sensitive personal information** — we do not use sensitive personal information beyond the purposes permitted without a right to limit.
- **Non-discrimination** — we will not discriminate against you for exercising your rights.
- Exercise rights through an **authorized agent**, subject to verification.

### How to exercise your rights

Submit a request to **privacy@cycleforge.ai**. We will verify your identity before responding and will reply within the timeframes required by applicable law. If your request concerns **Customer Data** (for example, you are an end-customer/buyer of a reseller), we will refer your request to the relevant Customer as controller, or act on the Customer's instructions, as appropriate.

---

## 11. Cookies & Analytics

We use cookies and similar technologies on the Site and Service in two categories:

- **Essential** — strictly necessary for authentication, session integrity, security (including fraud prevention and rate-limiting), and core functionality. These cannot be disabled without breaking the Service and do not require consent.
- **Analytics** — provided through **PostHog Inc.**, used to understand product usage, funnels, performance, and session context on the Site and within the Service. Where required by law, analytics cookies are set **only with your consent**.

**How to opt out.** Where a consent banner or preferences control is presented, you can decline or withdraw consent for analytics at any time. You can also configure your browser to block or delete cookies, and use any "Do Not Track" or Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals your browser supports; we honor recognized opt-out preference signals where required. Disabling essential cookies may prevent the Service from functioning.

---

## 12. Children's Privacy

The Service is a business-to-business tool **not directed to children**. It is not intended for, and we do not knowingly collect personal information from, anyone under the age of **16** (or **18** where a higher age threshold applies under local law). If you believe a child has provided us with personal information, contact **privacy@cycleforge.ai** and we will delete it.

---

## 13. Changes to This Policy

We may update this Policy from time to time to reflect changes in our practices, technology, legal requirements, or sub-processors. When we make material changes, we will update the "Last updated" date above and provide notice through the Service, by email to the workspace's billing/admin contact, or by posting on the Site, as appropriate. Your continued use of the Service after the effective date of an updated Policy constitutes acceptance of the changes, except where additional consent is required by law.

---

## 14. Contact Us

For questions about this Policy or our privacy practices, or to exercise your rights, contact us at:

- **Privacy & data-subject requests:** privacy@cycleforge.ai
- **Data-protection contact / EU–UK representative (to be appointed if required):** dpo@cycleforge.ai
- **Security, vulnerability reports, and breach notifications:** security@cycleforge.ai
- **Legal notices:** legal@cycleforge.ai
- **Support:** hi@cycleforge.ai
- **Operating entity:** [LEGAL ENTITY NAME]
- **Postal address:** [REGISTERED ADDRESS]

This Policy is governed by the laws of **[GOVERNING-LAW STATE], USA**, without regard to its conflict-of-laws principles, as further set out in the Terms of Service.
